title: Autonomous Agent Principles: A Governance Framework for Earned Autonomy
description: Theory-level framework establishing how autonomous AI agents earn and exercise authority. Defines two core problems (autonomous reasoning and earned autonomy), three architectural principles (Commander's Intent, Observable Autonomy, Convergence Is Silence), and one emergent measurable property (Autonomous Reasoning Fidelity, ARF). Includes empirical evidence from a three-family silence-convergence test (Anthropic, xAI/Grok, Google/Gemini) and documented falsification findings that bound the convergence claim. Implementation-agnostic.
